mnemonics: FLAT NESTS: Flat transactions are like a simple, single-story building (one level), while Nested transactions are like a Russian nesting doll (transactions within transactions).
keyPoints: A flat transaction is a single, indivisible unit of work with a single BEGIN and COMMIT/ABORT.
A nested transaction allows a parent transaction to spawn child subtransactions, forming a hierarchy.
Only the top-level (root) parent transaction in a nested structure can commit; a child's commit is provisional until the parent commits.
commonMistakes: Assuming a nested transaction can independently commit its changes to the permanent database, when in reality only the root parent can.
Believing that aborting a child transaction always forces the entire parent transaction to abort, when some models allow for forward recovery or alternative subtransactions.
Confusing the nesting of transactions with the distribution of transactions across multiple sites; a flat transaction can be distributed, and a nested transaction can be local.
